story.intro: Your crew awakens in the observation deck of the *Cassandra*, a deep-space vessel that vanished 50 years ago. The ship's AI is fragmented, speaking in riddles. The hull groans. Something moves in the dark corridors. You have 60 minutes to restore power and escape before the ship's orbit decays.
story.outro: The emergency beacon fires. As the *Cassandra* fades into the void behind you, the AI's final words crackle through your comms: 'Thank you for setting me free.' The ship's lights wink out one by one.

puzzles.prompt: The navigation console displays a scrambled command. Decode it to learn your next move. The coded text reads: "QJMYUZQ FTQ MEFDMX YAZUFAD"
hints: The message uses a simple letter-shift cipher — every letter has moved the same number of steps in the alphabet.
Try shifting each letter backward by 12 positions.
Shifting each letter back by 12 reveals: "EXAMINE THE ASTRAL MONITOR".

puzzles.prompt: The astral monitor flickers to life. The AI's broken voice recites: 'I exist in all worlds yet touch none. I hold infinite depth yet weigh nothing. The closer you draw, the less I show. I am the canvas of all that was and will be. What am I?'
hints: Think of what surrounds the ship and what the monitor displays.
It is both physical and conceptual—a container, not a thing.
The word you seek rhymes with 'place' and defines the void itself.

puzzles.prompt: The reactor chamber hums. Readings scroll across its display: 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, ?. What is the next value?
hints: Each step builds upon the previous one in a consistent way.
Watch how the numbers relate to each other—multiplication, not addition.
Each number is double the one before it. Continue the doubling.

puzzles.prompt: The captain's log contains crew assignments from the final day. Four officers—Vex, Kor, Mira, and Chen—each had a station: Navigation, Engineering, Medical, Science. Clues: (1) Vex was not at Navigation or Medical. (2) The officer at Engineering was not Kor or Mira. (3) Chen was at Science or Medical. (4) Mira was at Navigation or Engineering. (5) If Kor was at Navigation, then Vex was at Science. From these facts, who was assigned to Medical?
hints: Start by eliminating impossible pairings using clues (1) and (4).
Clue (2) means either Vex or Chen was in Engineering; clue (3) heavily constrains Chen.
If Chen is at Science, clue (3) is violated. So Chen must be at Medical.

puzzles.prompt: The failsafe vault contains one final encoded instruction. Decode the message to trigger the emergency beacon. The coded text reads: "LNETGLEP ESP PXPCRPYNJ MPLNZY"
hints: The message uses a simple letter-shift cipher — every letter has moved the same number of steps in the alphabet.
Try shifting each letter backward by 11 positions.
Shifting each letter back by 11 reveals: "ACTIVATE THE EMERGENCY BEACON".

puzzles.prompt: The diagnostic terminal shows cascade failure warnings. Power is draining from five systems at different rates. System A loses 3 units per cycle. System B loses 6 units per cycle. System C loses 12 units per cycle. All systems started at 240 units. After 5 cycles, what is the total remaining power across all three systems?
hints: Compute the total loss for each system separately, then combine results.
System A loses 15 units after 5 cycles, System B loses 30, System C loses 60.
